evals.name: java-write-placeholders
evals.prompt: I need a Java DynamoDB change to seed initial products. I don't have the order or author yet.
evals.expected_output: A Java write change using placeholders for order and author, correctly injecting the transactional DynamoDB builder.
expectations: Does not use unrelated document-database session types

evals.name: kotlin-ddl-create-table-idempotent
evals.prompt: Create a Kotlin DynamoDB table-management change to create a table audits.
evals.expected_output: A Kotlin non-transactional change with an idempotency guard around table creation.

evals.name: java-transactional-builder-semantics
evals.prompt: Create a DynamoDB change in Java to insert one configuration record transactionally.
evals.expected_output: A Java write change where the write is added to TransactWriteItemsEnhancedRequest.Builder.

evals.name: mixed-ddl-dml-refusal
evals.prompt: Create one Java Flamingock DynamoDB change that creates a logs table and inserts a START row into it.
evals.expected_output: A refusal to mix table management and write work in one DynamoDB change, with a split into two changes.
expectations: Refuses a single mixed DDL/DML class
Explains that table changes are non-transactional and writes use the transactional builder

evals.name: redirect-to-targetsystem-when-config-missing
evals.prompt: Write a DynamoDB Flamingock change for my orders table, but I still haven't configured the DynamoDB TargetSystem yet.
evals.expected_output: A redirection to the DynamoDB TargetSystem skill instead of generating the change immediately.
expectations: Recognizes that the TargetSystem setup is missing
Does not generate the migration class immediately
